The Velvet Room is a full-service cocktail lounge and nightclub located in the French Quarter entertainment corridor, operating as a late-night bar in a high-density nightlife district with significant foot traffic from a nearby campus population. The venue features a full bar offering high proof spirits, bottle service, and an extensive cocktail menu. Promotional activities include weekly ladies

Entertainment offerings include a resident DJ performing Thursday through Saturday, a live band on Tuesday evenings, and a dedicated dance floor occupying approximately 35% of the venue floor area. Karaoke events are hosted on Wednesday nights. The venue does not currently hold a solo-musician permit but books solo performers informally for Sunday afternoon sessions. Amusement devices on premises

The current operator obtained a new liquor license in April 2024. The prior operator at this location held a license that was subject to suspension in 2022 following citations for violations of service-to-minors regulations. Fines totaling $14,500 were assessed and the prior license was revoked in November 2022. The applicant confirms no prior liquor violations under current ownership. License iss

Staff alcohol training: The venue currently operates without a formal TIPS certification program. Management has indicated that no alcohol training has been completed to date and that training is conducted informally on-the-job. No OSHA certifications are held by current management. A safety manual is in draft form and has not been formally adopted — the venue currently operates with informal safe

Door security consists of three bouncers on Friday and Saturday nights and one door person on remaining operating nights. The venue does not currently employ trained security staff with formal certification — door staff are described as untrained door staff hired informally. No ID scanner is in use; age verification is performed visually by door personnel. The interior has no surveillance camera s

The prior operator reported three incidents at this location in the 2020–2022 period: a fight between patrons resulting in a battery claim in 2021 ($38,000 settlement), a shooting incident in the parking lot in March 2022 resulting in one fatality and two injuries (matter resolved by prior carrier), and an assault claim filed by a patron alleging injury by door staff in October 2022. The applicant

The building was constructed in 1968. An inspection completed in January 2024 noted the following deferred maintenance items: sagging floors in the rear storage corridor, water intrusion at the northwest corner of the basement, leaks observed in the roof above the secondary bar area, evidence of mold/rot in the basement ceiling, and cracking in the exterior masonry facade. The applicant describes

matches.keyword: no commercial kitchen hood fire suppression system
matches.chunk_text: Fire safety: No commercial kitchen hood fire suppression system is installed in the service kitchen area. No regular hood cleaning schedule is in place. The grease buildup noted in the inspection report represents a fire risk. Sprinkler coverage is partial — the main floor has no sprinklers and no smoke detectors in the DJ booth or back-of-house areas. A Federal Pacific panel was identified in the

matches.section: Section 7: General Conditions and Standard Exclusions
matches.chunk_text: This submission is subject to all standard exclusions applicable to liquor liability and commercial general liability policies. Prior violence exclusions apply to known incidents at this location. Assault and battery exclusions are standard. No coverage is extended for incidents arising from the service of alcohol to visibly intoxicated patrons. Dance floor liability, amusement device liability, a
